Name: dynamic_cache_partitioning_prefetching
Title: Dynamic Cache Partitioning and Prefetching for Efficient KV-Cache Updates in Long-Context LLMs
Short Hypothesis: Intelligently partitioning the KV-cache and prefetching relevant data blocks based on predicted attention patterns can significantly reduce memory-bandwidth bottlenecks and improve overall inference throughput.
Related Work: BitDecoding leverages Tensor Cores for efficient low-bit KV-cache decoding but does not address memory-bandwidth utilization during KV-cache updates. Other related works focus on quantization and sparsity techniques, but none have explored dynamic cache partitioning and prefetching based on predicted attention patterns.
Abstract: The rise of long-context Large Language Models (LLMs) amplifies memory and bandwidth demands during autoregressive decoding, as the Key-Value (KV) cache grows with each generated token. Existing systems suffer from memory-bandwidth bottlenecks during KV-cache updates, limiting overall inference throughput. We propose a novel approach to optimize memory-bandwidth utilization by introducing dynamic cache partitioning and prefetching strategies. Our method intelligently partitions the KV-cache and prefetches relevant data blocks based on predicted attention patterns, significantly reducing memory-bandwidth bottlenecks. We implement our approach as a set of CUDA kernels and evaluate its performance on modern GPU architectures. Our experiments demonstrate substantial improvements in inference throughput, with measured speedups of up to 3.5× on LLaMA-3.1-8B with a 128K context. We provide detailed profiling results, including occupancy, achieved bandwidth, and warp stall reasons, to validate the hardware-level efficiency of our approach.
Experiments: Implement dynamic cache partitioning and prefetching strategies as CUDA kernels.
Evaluate the performance of our approach on modern GPU architectures (e.g., Ampere, Hopper) using LLaMA-3.1-8B with a 128K context.
Measure inference throughput, p50/p99 latency, and energy per token.
Collect profiling metrics, including occupancy, achieved bandwidth, warp stall reasons, and arithmetic intensity.
Compare our approach against baseline systems (e.g., BitDecoding, FP16 FlashDecoding-v2) to validate the improvements in memory-bandwidth utilization and overall inference throughput.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The effectiveness of the prefetching strategy depends on the accuracy of attention pattern predictions, which may vary across different models and contexts.
Dynamic cache partitioning may introduce additional overhead, potentially offsetting the gains from reduced memory-bandwidth bottlenecks.
The approach may require fine-tuning for different GPU architectures to achieve optimal performance.
